messages.content: holy shit i actually did it. all 6 tracks are mixed and i just sent the whole thing off to Juno Audio this morning
That's huge! How are you feeling about the final mixes? And Juno Audio - that's the mastering studio you were looking into?

yeah exactly, Juno's the one. cost is steep though - 1200 total for the whole EP but honestly after listening to their portfolio i know it's worth it. the way they handle ambient stuff is just... chef's kiss
That investment sounds smart for something as important as your debut EP. What's their timeline looking like?
they said 3 to 4 weeks which puts us right around new year. perfect timing actually because i can use the holidays to prep all the visual stuff and get the bandcamp page ready
Nice timing! It must feel surreal to have Tidal at this stage after working on it for months.

dude it really is. like back in october i only had 4 tracks and they felt so rough. now listening to the final mixes... mira's vocals on track 3 still give me chills every time. and the field recordings from the coast trip actually work as transitions between the heavier sections
Those coastal field recordings were such a good call. How are you feeling about the financial side of things with the mastering cost?

honestly? the patreon growth has been a lifesaver. i'm at 168 patrons now so that's covering most of my base expenses. plus with the new commission rate at 600 per piece instead of 400, one good client project basically covers the mastering. feels good to invest properly in the music for once instead of cutting corners
That's such a good position to be in - being able to invest in quality without stressing about the money. Sounds like all the pieces are coming together for the EP launch.
exactly. now i just have to resist the urge to obsessively check my email every 20 minutes waiting for updates from the mastering engineer haha
Ha! That's going to be the hardest part. Maybe channel that nervous energy into prepping the visual assets and release strategy?

summary: Sam celebrates completing all 6 tracks for their debut EP 'Tidal' and sending them to Juno Audio for mastering. They discuss the $1,200 mastering cost, 3-4 week timeline, and how their growing Patreon and increased commission rates are making it financially feasible to invest properly in the music.
